claimSummary: Springfield says it has identified utility-owned service line materials, asks pre-1989 homes to self-identify customer-owned materials, and publishes an interactive inventory map.
reviewerNote: City Utilities lead and copper rule page reviewed manually for pre-1989 self-identification and map workflow.

claimSummary: Springfield says more than 38000 customer service lines have been identified, older homes still need to report materials, and City Utilities provides free neighborhood assistance while self-reporting and map cleanup continue.
reviewerNote: City Utilities update page reviewed manually for current progress and neighborhood assistance language.

claimSummary: Columbia says its lead compliance program includes a searchable assessment map and service line inventory information for the utility system.
reviewerNote: City of Columbia lead compliance page reviewed manually for map and utility-side inventory details.

claimSummary: Columbia asks customers with unknown private-side materials to complete an online service line identification survey and explains how to inspect the line.
reviewerNote: City of Columbia lead compliance page reviewed manually for survey and step-by-step inspection guidance.

claimSummary: Independence says it is completing a comprehensive lead and copper inventory program and publishes a current water service line inventory PDF for residents.
reviewerNote: City of Independence lead and copper rule page reviewed manually for current PDF inventory access.

claimSummary: Independence says customers with galvanized requiring replacement or unknown lines receive written notices and can request staff help to identify service line materials instead of relying on a full interactive lookup.
reviewerNote: City of Independence lead and copper rule page reviewed manually for written notice and staff-help guidance.

claimSummary: M.U.D. says customers can use its lead service line information and map tools to check whether a property has a lead line or lead-status-unknown record.
reviewerNote: M.U.D. lead information page reviewed manually for map and eligibility lookup wording.

claimSummary: M.U.D. gives unknown-status customers a survey path, instructions to identify service line material, and access to free pitchers or sample kits through partner programs.
reviewerNote: M.U.D. lead-status-unknown page reviewed manually for survey, pitcher, and sample-kit guidance.

claimSummary: M.U.D. says its multi-year program started in 2024, uses a lead service line map to determine eligibility, and replaces customer-owned lead service lines free of charge.
reviewerNote: M.U.D. replacement program page reviewed manually for 2024 launch timing and free replacement coverage.

claimSummary: M.U.D. says customer-owned lead service lines are replaced free of charge, ties eligibility to its program rollout, and notes an average replacement value of about $8000.
reviewerNote: M.U.D. replacement program page reviewed manually for cost language.

claimSummary: Iowa American Water says customers can use an interactive web-based map to view utility-side and customer-side service line materials and self-report unknown lines through the same workflow.
reviewerNote: Iowa American Water lead and drinking water page reviewed manually for map and self-report workflow.

claimSummary: Iowa American Water says customers with unknown lines received letters to self-identify materials, customers with lead or galvanized lines received notices about exposure reduction and replacement plans, and about 1300 customer-owned lines had already been replaced by November 2024.
reviewerNote: Iowa American Water press release reviewed manually for notification language and replacement progress.

claimSummary: Iowa American Water says it is replacing lead and galvanized service lines through an ongoing program, asks customers to use the map and self-report workflow to identify remaining lines, and reported about 1300 customer-owned line replacements by November 2024.
reviewerNote: Iowa American Water lead and drinking water page plus progress release reviewed manually for program details.

claimSummary: Iowa American Water says lead and galvanized lines are being replaced at no direct cost to customers under the current program and reported about 1300 completed customer-owned replacements by November 2024.
reviewerNote: Iowa American Water press release reviewed manually for cost language and replacement progress.
